description: Japanese organized crime syndicate dating back to the 17th century, known for strict codes of conduct, ritual practices like yubitsume, and full-body tattoos. It engages in extortion, drug trafficking, money laundering, and other illegal activities. Membership peaked in 1963 at 184,100 but declined to about 22,000 by 2022. The group remains active and sophisticated, with international operations.

country_links.context: The Yakuza has territory in Mexico.
